title: Extension of W_0 to W(Pi) and the u_1-translation identity asserted, not proven

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap — extension of W_0 to W(Pi) and the off-mirabolic u_1-translation identity.**

The solution asserts W_0(g) = int_{N_n} 1_{K_n}(xg) psi(x) dx 'extends, via the theory of the Kirillov model, to an element of W(Pi,psi^{-1}) on G_{n+1}', and uses

  W_0(g t_Q) = psi(-g_{nn}) W_0(g d_Q^{-1}),   t_Q = d_Q^{-1} u_Q = u_1 d_Q^{-1},  u_1 = I+E_{n,n+1} in N_{n+1}.

The existence of the extension is plausible (W_0 is compactly supported mod N_n, hence in C_c^infty(N_n\G_n; psi), contained in the Kirillov model restricted to the mirabolic P_{n+1}). However: (1) the precise statement that W_0 arises is not given; (2) the right-translation identity by u_1 in N_{n+1} is where the GL_{n+1} structure genuinely enters. Right multiplication by N_{n+1} does NOT act by a character in general; the identity must be derived from the explicit Kirillov realization (mirabolic action Pi(u_1)). It is asserted without proof.

**Suggested fix:** Write W_0 explicitly in the Kirillov model of Pi and compute the right-u_1 translate from the mirabolic action, citing Bernstein-Zelevinsky / Matringe.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
**Resolved.** I replaced the two asserted claims with full proofs (two new lemmas, `lem:extend` and `lem:u1translate`), inserted in the "Choice of W and V" section before their first use.

**(1) Extension of W_0 to W(Pi,psi^{-1}).** Previously asserted via "the theory of the Kirillov model." Now Lemma (Extension via Kirillov model) states precisely: for any f in S(N_n\\G_n; psi^{-1}) (locally constant, left (N_n,psi^{-1})-equivariant, compact support mod N_n), there is W in W(Pi,psi^{-1}) with W(diag(g,1)) = f(g). This is the Bernstein–Zelevinsky / JPSS-83 Kirillov-model theorem: W(Pi,psi^{-1}) restricted to the mirabolic P_{n+1} contains S(N_n\\G_n; psi^{-1}). I verify W_0 of (eq:W0) lies in this space (locally constant, left (N_n,psi^{-1})-equivariant by x->nx, supported on N_n K_n with compact transversal (N_n∩K_n)\\K_n), so a genuine G_{n+1}-extension exists and restricts to (eq:W0) on diag(G_n,1). Added citation JPSS83 (Rankin–Selberg convolutions, Amer. J. Math. 105 (1983)).

**(2) The u_1-translation identity.** The critic correctly noted that right multiplication by N_{n+1} does NOT act by a character in general, so the old one-line assertion was unjustified. The correct mechanism is conjugation, not a right-character: I prove

  W(diag(g,1) u_1 h) = psi(-g_{nn}) W(diag(g,1) h)  for all W in W(Pi,psi^{-1}), all h in G_{n+1}.

Proof: set n_g := diag(g,1) u_1 diag(g,1)^{-1}. A direct column computation (verified symbolically in sympy for n=3, general entries) gives n_g = I + sum_{i=1}^n g_{in} E_{i,n+1} in N_{n+1}. The generic character of N_{n+1} is u |-> psi(sum_{i=1}^n u_{i,i+1}), depending only on superdiagonal entries. Among the nonzero entries (n_g)_{i,n+1}=g_{in}, the ONLY one on the superdiagonal {(i,i+1)} is (n,n+1)=g_{nn}; the entries (i,n+1) with i<n lie strictly above the superdiagonal. Hence psi^{-1}(n_g) = psi(-g_{nn}). Then diag(g,1)u_1 = n_g diag(g,1), so by LEFT (N_{n+1},psi^{-1})-equivariance of W,
  W(diag(g,1)u_1 h) = W(n_g diag(g,1) h) = psi^{-1}(n_g) W(diag(g,1) h) = psi(-g_{nn}) W(diag(g,1)h).
This needs no explicit Kirillov realization of the mirabolic action — moving u_1 to the left of diag(g,1) turns it into an N_{n+1}-element whose generic-character value is psi(-g_{nn}).

Applying with h=d_Q^{-1} (and diag(g,1)d_Q^{-1}=diag(g d_Q^{-1},1) staying in diag(G_n,1), where the extension restricts to (eq:W0)) and t_Q=u_1 d_Q^{-1} gives exactly W_0(g t_Q)=psi(-g_{nn})W_0(g d_Q^{-1}), which feeds (eq:rel1).

**Verification.** Symbolically checked (sympy): t_Q = d_Q^{-1}u_Q = u_1 d_Q^{-1} with u_1=I+E_{n,n+1}; diag(g,1)u_1 diag(g,1)^{-1} = I + sum g_{in}E_{i,n+1}; its only superdiagonal entry is g_{nn}; diag(g,1)d_Q^{-1}=diag(g d_Q^{-1},1). All environments balanced, all \\ref/\\eqref defined; latexmk not installed in workspace so compilation was skipped.

Both sub-gaps of q2-5 are now proven, not asserted.
